name: Shahi Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 t Garam masala (commercial mix or homemade blend)
Grind together 5 cloves, 5 cardamom pods, 1 cinnamon stick, 1 bay leaf, pinch of mace until powdery
1/4 t Red chili powder or cayenne pepper
1 lb Boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Place cashew nuts in a small bowl and cover with hot water. Soak for 30 minutes and drain most of the water.
2. Blend the nuts until they form a smooth paste that retains some texture.
3. Heat oil in a saucepan or wok. Sauté onions until golden brown.
4. Add half a teaspoon of garam masala powder and "splutter fry" it. Set aside the remainder for garnishing.
5. Quickly add garlic, ginger, red chili powder, coriander powder (if using), and turmeric powder to the pan. Stir well.
6. Add chicken and mix until the meat is sealed with spices, about 2 minutes.
7. Mix in tomatoes and simmer until the chicken is cooked through.
8. Add cashew paste and heavy cream. Bring mixture to a boil.
9. If needed, adjust consistency by adding water for a smooth, slightly thick sauce.
10. Turn off heat and allow oil to float on top of the dish.
11. Add raisins and garnish with remaining garam masala and cardamom powder.
12. Serves 2 as a main dish or 4 if served with other dishes.
